lede: A look at February 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Millie Bobby Brown, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Killing of Mya Thwe Thwe Khine
events.desc: Mya Thwe Thwe Khine, a 19-year-old protester, becomes the first known casualty of anti-coup protests that formed in response to the 2021 Myanmar coup d'état.

events.title: Belitung shipwreck
events.desc: The debut exhibition of the Belitung shipwreck, containing the largest collection of Tang dynasty artifacts found in one location, begins in Singapore.

events.title: Methane
events.desc: A methane explosion in a coal mine near Nueva Rosita, Mexico, kills 65 miners.

events.title: Executive Order 9066
events.desc: Executive Order 9066, which led to the relocation of Japanese Americans to internment camps, is rescinded by President Gerald Ford's Proclamation 4417.

events.title: Phạm Ngọc Thảo
events.desc: Colonel Phạm Ngọc Thảo of the Army of the Republic of Vietnam, and a communist spy of the North Vietnamese Viet Minh, along with Generals Lâm Văn Phát and Trần Thiện Khiêm, all Catholics, attempt a coup against the military junta of the Buddhist Nguyễn Khánh.

events.title: Betty Friedan
events.desc: The publication of Betty Friedan's The Feminine Mystique reawakens the feminist movement in the United States as women's organizations and consciousness raising groups spread.

events.title: Conference of Youth and Students of Southeast Asia Fighting for Freedom and Independence
events.desc: The Conference of Youth and Students of Southeast Asia Fighting for Freedom and Independence convenes in Calcutta.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Nearly 250 Japanese warplanes attack the northern Australian city of Darwin, killing 243 people.

events.title: Yekatit 12
events.desc: Yekatit 12: During a public ceremony at the Viceregal Palace (the former Imperial residence) in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, two Ethiopian nationalists of Eritrean origin attempt to kill viceroy Rodolfo Graziani with a number of grenades.

events.title: Austin, Texas
events.desc: In Austin, Texas, the newly formed Texas state government is officially installed. The Republic of Texas government officially transfers power to the State of Texas government following the annexation of Texas by the United States.

births.name: Álvaro Obregón
births.line: Mexican general and politician, 39th President of Mexico (died 1928)

deaths.text: José Mojica Marins, Brazilian filmmaker, actor, composer, screenwriter, and television horror host

observances.text: Christian feast day: Lucy Yi Zhenmei (one of Martyrs of Guizhou)
